name: Friedman Vartolo LLP
description_short: Friedman Vartolo LLP is a New York-based law firm specializing in bankruptcy, foreclosure, and default servicing litigation founded in 2016 by partners Adam Friedman and Ralph Vartolo.
description_long:
Friedman Vartolo LLP was founded in 2016 to address the need for premium legal services in the default servicing industry. The firm is led by partners Adam Friedman and Ralph Vartolo, who built the practice around acquiring skilled professionals and developing new talent through rigorous training programs. The firm operates from New York and maintains an active web presence documenting their legal insights and case outcomes.

The firm offers comprehensive legal services across multiple practice areas including bankruptcy, foreclosure defense, litigation, evictions, real estate transactions and REO services, and title curative work. They handle both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy matters as part of their broader default servicing practice. Beyond direct legal services, they publish educational content through "The Weekly Friedman" podcast series featuring attorneys discussing amicus briefs, appellate decisions, and regulatory updates like Department of Financial Services housing counselor requirements.

Friedman Vartolo distinguishes itself through emphasis on workplace culture and attorney development. The firm explicitly promotes a modern, casual work atmosphere designed to support work-life balance and minimize stress associated with legal practice. They highlight collaborative effort, diversity of ideas, and values of diligence, determination, and communication. The firm actively recruits talent and has published multiple episodes of legal commentary addressing recent case law and regulatory changes affecting their practice areas.

As a law firm rather than a consumer financial service provider, Friedman Vartolo serves clients seeking bankruptcy representation and default servicing legal counsel, not individuals seeking personal financing solutions. The firm's focus on attorney hiring, training, and workplace culture suggests strength in legal execution but no independent assessment of client outcomes is available from the website. Bankruptcy services appear to be one component of a broader default servicing practice oriented toward institutional clients and creditors rather than consumer debtors.
pros: Founded in 2016 with dedicated focus on default servicing legal needs
Partners with track record: Adam Friedman and Ralph Vartolo established reputation in foreclosure and bankruptcy law
Publishes regular legal commentary through 'The Weekly Friedman' podcast addressing recent appellate decisions and regulatory changes
Offers comprehensive bankruptcy services alongside complementary practices (foreclosure, litigation, evictions, title work)
Explicit commitment to attorney development through rigorous training and professional growth programs
Work culture focused on work-life balance and minimizing attorney stress
Recently documented appellate victory in FLA Mortgage Capital 1 LLC v. Unknown Heirs case showing litigation success
cons: Law firm structure means high hourly rates typical of bankruptcy counsel; not accessible to all debt situations
Website URL contains error parameter, suggesting technical issues or incomplete website maintenance
Limited transparency on client outcomes, success rates, or specific bankruptcy filing statistics
Focus appears oriented toward default servicing and institutional clients rather than individual consumer debtors seeking bankruptcy relief
No pricing information, fee structures, or initial consultation details published on website
best_for: New York homeowners facing foreclosure seeking litigation representation
Individuals requiring Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy counsel in New York with adequate resources for attorney fees
Property owners dealing with title issues and real estate transactions requiring legal expertise
Institutional clients and creditors requiring default servicing legal representation
diagnosis: Friedman Vartolo LLP is best for New York residents requiring bankruptcy or foreclosure legal representation who can afford attorney fees and prefer a firm with documented appellate experience and modern work culture. The primary caveat is that this is a law firm serving institutional and individual clients with adequate resources; consumers seeking affordable bankruptcy assistance or debt relief should explore non-profit credit counseling or HUD-approved housing counselors first.
services: Appellate representation and amicus brief preparation
Regulatory compliance guidance (DFS housing counselor coordination)
